description: Canonical test vectors for the SEP 1v1 Soroban contract (`contracts/sep-oneonone`). Single source of truth for the contract's ABI: every error code, IC count, validation rule, wire field is documented and CI-asserted by the inline `test_vectors_consistency` test. 1v1 is the smallest of the per-type contracts: immutable two-party group, no update path, no deactivation, no quorum, no admin tree, tier hardcoded to 0, single Membership VK + single Create VK (no per-tier arrays).
architecture_note: This contract is the per-type 1v1 contract — fourth in the per-type family (Anarchy, OneOnOne, Democracy, Oligarchy). The `group_type` discriminator is implicit in the contract address; no `group_type` field in storage. The 1v1 contract is the only one without an `update_commitment` entrypoint by design — once a 1v1 group is created its commitment is sealed forever (the on-chain entry only ages out via TTL). It is also the only one without a `tier` parameter on `create_group` (tier=0 hardcoded for 1v1 per design §6.4.1) and without a `tier` field on `CommitmentEntry` (the contract address fixes the tier; storing a constant is noise).

error_codes.description: Numeric values of `Error` variants (`#[contracterror] #[repr(u32)]`). Number alignment with sibling per-type contracts is intentional: `7 = InvalidProof` everywhere, `26 = InvalidPoint` everywhere, etc. Gaps in the numbering (3, 6, 8, 11, 16-25, 27-30) are reserved by sibling contracts for variants 1v1 doesn't need (`InvalidTier`, `GroupInactive`, `InvalidEpoch`, `InvalidThreshold`, etc.); holding the alignment makes cross-contract error-code lookups consistent.

vectors.name: GroupCountLimitReached
vectors.note: 1v1's analogue of TierGroupLimitReached; renamed because there's a single counter, not per-tier

vectors.name: AdminOnly
vectors.note: create_group in restricted mode rejects non-admin callers

not_declared_aligned_with_siblings.name: Reserved3
not_declared_aligned_with_siblings.reason: reserved for sibling-contract numbering alignment; no current use in any per-type contract

not_declared_aligned_with_siblings.name: InvalidTier
not_declared_aligned_with_siblings.reason: no tier parameter; tier hardcoded to 0

not_declared_aligned_with_siblings.name: InvalidEpoch
not_declared_aligned_with_siblings.reason: no update path, no checked_add overflow to surface

not_declared_aligned_with_siblings.name: OneOnOneImmutable
not_declared_aligned_with_siblings.reason: 1v1's update path doesn't exist; the rejection happens by absence of the entrypoint, not by error

tier.description: 1v1 hardcodes tier=0 (Small, depth 5, capacity 32). The contract has no `tier` parameter on any entrypoint and no `tier` field on stored entries. Listed for reference and parallel with sibling contracts.
tier.rationale: 1v1 holds exactly 2 members at creation. Allowing tier 1/2 would let the group support up to 256 / 2048 members at the contract level — but the OneOnOneCreateCircuit enforces 'exactly 2 non-zero leaves' in-circuit, so any tier larger than Small would just waste tree depth. Hardcoding tier=0 at the contract level is honest about the constraint.

max_groups.description: Cross-group cap enforced by create_group: GroupCount must be < MAX_GROUPS, else GroupCountLimitReached. Single counter (no per-tier array) because tier is fixed at 0.
max_groups.rule: GroupCount MUST be < 10000 at create_group; exceeded → GroupCountLimitReached
max_groups.operational_note: GroupCount is monotonic increment-only (no decrement path since no `deactivate_group`). At testnet scale, fine. At production scale, 10k 1v1 groups per contract instance is restrictive — two parties holding multiple short conversations would burn through the budget. The right answer is probably 'deploy a fresh contract instance and route by deployment address' rather than re-introduce decrementing. Out of scope for v0; documented for visibility.

vk_kind_enum.description: Two VK kinds — Membership + Create. Both are stored as single VKs (not per-tier arrays) because tier is hardcoded to 0. Membership is the standard 3-IC `(commitment, epoch)` shape used at `verify_membership`. Create is the OneOnOneCreateCircuit VK that binds the 'exactly 2 non-zero leaves' invariant in-circuit at create time. This parallels sep-oligarchy's distinct Create VK (which binds the verbose §4.8 tuple); the pattern is: when a contract needs to enforce a creation-time invariant beyond 'you know a leaf,' that invariant gets its own circuit and VK.

vectors.name: Create
vectors.note: OneOnOneCreateCircuit. Same public-input shape as the Membership circuit — the 2-leaf invariant is enforced inside the circuit's witness, not in the public-input layout. Future ceremony work may add additional public inputs (e.g., a domain-tag scalar to disambiguate the two circuits and prevent VK confusion). v0 keeps the 3-IC shape to minimize Phase A blockers.

vk_kind_enum.ic_ordering_canonical: MUST match the OneOnOneCreateCircuit and standard MembershipCircuit allocation orders. Pinned for cross-implementation reference.

create_group_validation.description: Per-call validation in `create_group`. Single-type contract — no per-type dispatch. No tier parameter, no member_count parameter (always 2 by definition). The Create-circuit verifier opens (commitment, epoch=0) — 1v1 groups always start at epoch 0 and never advance.
rules: group_id MUST NOT already be assigned; collision → GroupAlreadyExists
commitment MUST be a canonical Fr encoding; non-canonical → InvalidCommitmentEncoding
GroupCount MUST be < MAX_GROUPS (10000); exceeded → GroupCountLimitReached
if RestrictedMode is true: caller MUST equal Admin, else AdminOnly (caller.require_auth() runs first)
public_inputs.commitment MUST equal `commitment` parameter; mismatch → PublicInputsMismatch
proof MUST verify under the Create VK against (commitment, 0); failure → InvalidProof
proof_hash(proof) MUST NOT be in UsedProof storage; collision → ProofReplay

front_running_surface.description: create_group is replayable by an observer of a prover's transaction (caller is NOT bound into the Create proof; only the auth-gate runs against caller). This is benign griefing — the attacker can only create the group with the prover's same commitment under their own caller; the legitimate creator's subsequent submission fails with GroupAlreadyExists or ProofReplay; there is no per-creator on-chain privilege to be stolen. Mitigation: the legitimate creator picks a different group_id and resubmits. Same shape as create_group on the other three per-type contracts — distinct from the post-#153 deactivate_group pattern (which was uniquely dangerous because the state delta was irreversible).
front_running_surface.post_postmortem_153_note: 1v1 ships without `deactivate_group` from the start (see docs/postmortem-deactivate-group-frontrun.md). 1v1 groups age out via storage TTL; there is no on-chain 'leave' event.

storage_layout.description: Storage entries written by the contract. Per-type 1v1 means `group_type` is implicit in the contract address (not a stored field). `tier` and `member_count` are not stored either: both are constants for 1v1 (0 and 2 respectively).

fields.name: epoch
fields.rule: always 0 for 1v1; the contract has no `update_commitment` entrypoint to advance it

removed_vs_per_type_template.rationale: single-type contract; the contract address IS the discriminator
no deactivate path, no code can set it false
always 0 by definition; clients reading get_commitment know the tier from the contract address
always 2 by definition; storing a constant is noise

data_keys.name: Admin
data_keys.note: contract admin; required for update_vk and set_restricted_mode

data_keys.name: RestrictedMode
data_keys.note: when true, only the admin may call create_group; toggled by set_restricted_mode (admin-only). Default false.

data_keys.name: MembershipVK
data_keys.note: Membership-circuit VK (single, not per-tier); 3 IC points; used at verify_membership

data_keys.name: CreateVK
data_keys.note: OneOnOneCreate-circuit VK (single, not per-tier); 3 IC points; used at create_group

data_keys.name: Group(group_id)
data_keys.note: current group state; immutable after create

data_keys.name: UsedProof(proof_hash)
data_keys.note: global-nullifier scope; only create_group writes here

data_keys.name: GroupCount
data_keys.note: active group count; checked against MAX_GROUPS (10000) in create_group. Monotonic increment-only since 1v1 has no deactivate path; the cap is a one-way ratchet. Operationally fine at testnet scale.

admin_entrypoints.description: Admin-gated entrypoints. All require admin.require_auth(); panic on unauth. There is no separate `set_admin` entrypoint — admin is fixed at __constructor.

vectors.name: update_vk

args.name: new_vk
args.rule: IC length == 3 (both kinds); subgroup-valid; else InvalidVkLength / InvalidPoint

vectors.purpose: rotate Membership or Create VK — used for ceremony-rotation. NO `tier` parameter (tier hardcoded to 0).

vectors.name: set_restricted_mode
vectors.purpose: when restricted=true, create_group rejects non-admin callers with AdminOnly. Storage: DataKey::RestrictedMode (instance). Default value (key absent) is treated as false. Emits RestrictedModeChanged event for audit transparency.

queries.description: Read-only entrypoints. None require auth. All return Result<_, Error>.

vectors.name: verify_membership
vectors.note: returns Ok(false) on bad proof rather than InvalidProof — read-only verifier semantics. Verifies under the Membership VK (NOT the Create VK).

vectors.name: bump_group_ttl
vectors.note: permissionless TTL bump — the only ongoing lifecycle event for a 1v1 group

epoch_invariant.description: 1v1 epoch is always 0. The contract has no entrypoint that advances epoch. PublicInputs.epoch MUST be 0 at create_group and verify_membership; non-zero → PublicInputsMismatch.
epoch_invariant.rule: create_group rejects public_inputs.epoch != 0 with PublicInputsMismatch; verify_membership rejects the same with PublicInputsMismatch. There is no checked_add path that could overflow.

proof_replay_protection.description: Global nullifier scope. The nullifier is sha256(proof.a || proof.b || proof.c) over the 384-byte uncompressed proof bytes. Scope is global per contract: the same 384 bytes used at create_group on any group consume the nullifier.
rules: Once a proof's bytes have been recorded by record_proof at create_group, any subsequent create_group call with byte-identical proof bytes is rejected with ProofReplay.

TTL of UsedProof entries is LEDGER_BUMP (~30 days). After expiry the same proof bytes can be re-used; this is not a concern in practice because each create_group call requires a fresh 2-leaf tree (different group_id implies different commitment).

verify_membership does NOT consume nullifiers (read-only verifier semantics). 1v1 has no membership-VK-keyed state-changing entrypoint to replay the leaked proof against, which closes the post-#153 attack pattern by construction.

tests_to_implement.description: Inline test names that the `#[cfg(test)] mod test` block in `contracts/sep-oneonone/src/test.rs` MUST cover. The `test_vectors_consistency` test loads this very file and asserts the contract's `Error` enum / IC-point constants / MAX_GROUPS match the values pinned above. Every other entry in this list maps 1:1 to a `#[test] fn` in the inline module.
